ucs.title: [LLM] Evil Corp / Indrik Spider SocGholish (FakeUpdates) — browser → wscript JS payload → C2 child process
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical SocGholish 'fake browser update' drive-by chain operated by Evil Corp / Indrik Spider (Manatee Tempest, DEV-0243, UNC2165). After the user accepts a fake Chrome/Edge update on a compromised site, the browser spawns wscript.exe to run a heavily obfuscated .js loader from a user-writable directory; that wscript instance then launches PowerShell/cmd/rundll32 within seconds for staging (NetSupport RAT, Cobalt Strike, BLISTER) prior to BitPaymer/WastedLocker/LockBit deployment.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as wscriptTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=wscript.exe AND Processes.parent_process_name IN (chrome.exe, msedge.exe, firefox.exe, brave.exe, iexplore.exe) AND (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Temp\\*") AND (Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename process_id as wscript_pid, process as wscript_cmd | join type=inner host wscript_pid [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as childTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=wscript.exe AND Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe, pwsh.exe, cmd.exe, rundll32.exe, regsvr32.exe, mshta.exe) by host Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename parent_process_id as wscript_pid, process as child_cmd, process_name as child_proc ] | where (childTime - wscriptTime) < 300 | eval delaySec = childTime - wscriptTime | table wscriptTime childTime delaySec host user wscript_cmd child_proc child_cmd | sort - wscriptTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let Browsers = dynamic(["chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","iexplore.exe"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe","wmic.exe"]);
let WscriptStage = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName =~ "wscript.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (Browsers)
    | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.js[e]?\b"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\")
    | project WscriptTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              JsCmd = ProcessCommandLine, WscriptPid = ProcessId;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wscript.exe"
| join kind=inner WscriptStage on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.WscriptPid
| where Timestamp between (WscriptTime .. WscriptTime + 5m)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, WscriptTime),
          JsCmd, ChildProc = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let Browsers = dynamic([@"\chrome.exe", @"\msedge.exe", @"\firefox.exe", @"\brave.exe", @"\iexplore.exe"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic([@"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe", @"\cmd.exe", @"\rundll32.exe", @"\regsvr32.exe", @"\mshta.exe", @"\wmic.exe"]);
let WscriptStage = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\wscript.exe"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (Browsers)
    | where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\.js[e]?\b"
    | where CommandLine has_any (@"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\")
    | project WscriptTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              JsCmd = CommandLine, WscriptPid = NewProcessId;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName has_any (SuspiciousChildren)
| where ParentProcessName endswith @"\wscript.exe"
| join kind=inner WscriptStage on Computer, $left.ProcessId == $right.WscriptPid
| where TimeGenerated between (WscriptTime .. WscriptTime + 5m)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, WscriptTime),
          JsCmd, ChildProc = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: SocGholish FakeUpdates - Browser-Spawned wscript Executes JS from User-Writable Path
id: 9f6a6f00-1dc3-4e5e-a8a0-1b3a4d7e2f9a
status: experimental
description: Detects the Evil Corp / Indrik Spider (UNC2165, Manatee Tempest) SocGholish drive-by chain where a browser process launches wscript.exe to execute a JavaScript loader from Temp, Downloads, or AppData. Pre-cursor to NetSupport RAT, Cobalt Strike, and BitPaymer/WastedLocker/LockBit deployment.
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
references:
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0092/
    - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/socgholish-fake-update-framework
    - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/05/09/ransomware-as-a-service-understanding-the-cybercrime-gig-economy/
tags:
    - attack.initial-access
    - attack.t1189
    - attack.execution
    - attack.t1059.007
    - attack.t1204.001
    - attack.g0092
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_proc:
        Image|endswith: '\wscript.exe'
        ParentImage|endswith:
            - '\chrome.exe'
            - '\msedge.exe'
            - '\firefox.exe'
            - '\brave.exe'
            - '\iexplore.exe'
    selection_path:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
            - '\AppData\Roaming\'
            - '\Downloads\'
            - '\Temp\'
    selection_ext:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - '.js'
            - '.jse'
    condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate browser-launched JS via in-house web tooling (very rare)
    - User manually executing a JS file just downloaded (uncommon, behaviour-equivalent)
level: high
ucs.rationale: SocGholish is the documented Evil Corp / Indrik Spider initial-access framework — Mandiant, Microsoft (Manatee Tempest / DEV-0243) and CISA have all attributed FakeUpdates to this cluster. The browser→wscript.exe→JS-from-AppData→PowerShell/rundll32 chain is the operator's signature: legitimate web apps essentially never spawn wscript with a Temp-located JS, and the 5-minute child-process correlati

ucs.title: [LLM] TA505 / Cl0p MOVEit Transfer exploitation — w3wp.exe spawning shells & LEMURLOOT (human2.aspx) webshell drop
ucs.description: Hunts in-the-wild TA505 (Cl0p / GOLD DRAKE / Hive0065) post-exploitation of the MOVEit Transfer SQLi (CVE-2023-34362) and similar managed-file-transfer zero-days (Accellion FTA, GoAnywhere MFT). The detection looks for the IIS worker process w3wp.exe spawning recon/shell binaries OR creating the LEMURLOOT webshell artefacts (`human2.aspx`, `_human2.aspx`, `app_web_human2.*`) inside the MOVEit web root — both the textbook entry-point footprint Cl0p left in the May–June 2023 mass-exploitation campaign that affected 2,700+ orgs.
ucs.splunk: (`tstats` summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=w3wp.exe AND Processes.process_name IN (cmd.exe, powershell.exe, pwsh.exe, net.exe, net1.exe, whoami.exe, systeminfo.exe, cscript.exe, wscript.exe, nltest.exe, hostname.exe, ipconfig.exe, tasklist.exe, certutil.exe) by host Processes.user Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search parent_process_path="*MOVEit*" OR parent_process="*MOVEitTransfer*" | eval signal="w3wp_spawning_shell") | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path="*\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\*" AND (Filesystem.file_name IN (human2.aspx, _human2.aspx) OR Filesystem.file_name="app_web_human2*" OR (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" AND Filesystem.process_name=w3wp.exe)) by host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval signal="lemurloot_webshell_drop" ] | stats min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime values(signal) as signals values(process_name) as process_name values(process) as cmdline values(file_name) as file_name values(file_path) as file_path values(user) as user by host | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | sort - firstTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let MoveitWebroot = @"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\";
let LemurLootArtifacts = dynamic(["human2.aspx","_human2.aspx","human.aspx"]);
let ShellTools = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","ipconfig.exe","tasklist.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
union isfuzzy=true
( DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MOVEitTransfer", "MOVEit DMZ")
        or InitiatingProcessFolderPath has @"\MOVEit"
    | where FileName in~ (ShellTools)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Signal = "w3wp_spawning_shell",
              ChildProc = FileName, ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              FilePath = "", FileName = "", SHA256),
( DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified")
    | where FolderPath has MoveitWebroot
    | where FileName in~ (LemurLootArtifacts)
        or FileName startswith "app_web_human2"
        or (FileName endswith ".aspx" and InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Signal = "lemurloot_webshell_drop",
              ChildProc = "", ChildCmd = "",
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ParentPath = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
              FilePath = FolderPath, FileName, SHA256)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let MoveitWebroot = @"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\";
let LemurLootArtifacts = dynamic(["human2.aspx","_human2.aspx","human.aspx"]);
let ShellTools = dynamic([@"\cmd.exe", @"\powershell.exe", @"\pwsh.exe", @"\net.exe", @"\net1.exe", @"\whoami.exe", @"\systeminfo.exe", @"\cscript.exe", @"\wscript.exe", @"\nltest.exe", @"\hostname.exe", @"\ipconfig.exe", @"\tasklist.exe", @"\certutil.exe", @"\bitsadmin.exe"]);
union isfuzzy=true
( SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith @"\w3wp.exe"
    | where ParentProcessName has "MOVEit" or CommandLine has "MOVEit"
    | where NewProcessName has_any (ShellTools)
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              Signal = "w3wp_spawning_shell",
              ChildProc = NewProcessName, ChildCmd = CommandLine,
              Parent = ParentProcessName,
              FilePath = "", FileName = ""),
( ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFilePath has MoveitWebroot
    | where TargetFileName in~ (LemurLootArtifacts)
        or TargetFileName startswith "app_web_human2"
        or (TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" and ActingProcessName endswith @"\w3wp.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname, Account = ActorUsername,
              Signal = "lemurloot_webshell_drop",
              ChildProc = "", ChildCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = ActingProcessName,
              FilePath = TargetFilePath, FileName = TargetFileName)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: TA505/Cl0p has a multi-year track record of weaponising managed-file-transfer zero-days (Accellion FTA 2020, SolarWinds Serv-U, GoAnywhere MFT CVE-2023-0669, MOVEit Transfer CVE-2023-34362) and consistently drops the LEMURLOOT webshell — Mandiant publicly attributed the `human2.aspx` family to FIN11 / TA505. The two signals together (w3wp.exe spawning a recon/shell binary, AND an attacker-controll
